# Work Packages: PR Service

**Inputs**: Design documents from `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, contracts/PRServiceProtocol.swift
**Tests**: No automated tests (manual testing for v1 per constitution).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- All source file paths are relative to `Reppo/` (the Xcode project root).

## Path Conventions
- **Service protocol**: `Reppo/Core/Services/Protocols/PRServiceProtocol.swift`
- **Service implementation**: `Reppo/Core/Services/PRService.swift`
- **Repository protocols (existing)**: `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/`
- **Repository implementations (existing)**: `Reppo/Core/Repositories/`
- **Models (existing, read-only)**: `Reppo/Data/Models/`
- **Enums (existing, read-only)**: `Reppo/Data/Enums/`
- **Extensions (existing)**: `Reppo/Core/Extensions/UnitConversion.swift`
- **App entry point**: `Reppo/App/ReppoApp.swift`

---

## Work Package WP01: PRService Protocol + Repository Update (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create the PRServiceProtocol, supporting value types (PREvaluationResult, PRTableEntry), and add the missing `fetchBestEligibleSet()` method to SetRepositoryProtocol + SetRepository. This establishes the complete contract for the PR service and fills the repository gap needed for PR recomputation.
**Independent Test**: PRServiceProtocol compiles with all method signatures matching the contract in `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/contracts/PRServiceProtocol.swift`. SetRepository has `fetchBestEligibleSet()` that filters by eligibility criteria and returns the best candidate.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/tasks/WP01-protocol-and-repo-update.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 [P] Create `PREvaluationResult` struct and `PRTableEntry` struct in `Reppo/Core/Services/Protocols/PRServiceProtocol.swift`
- [x] T002 [P] Create `PRServiceProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Services/Protocols/PRServiceProtocol.swift` (same file as value types)
- [x] T003 Add `fetchBestEligibleSet()` method to `SetRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/SetRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T004 Implement `fetchBestEligibleSet()` in `SetRepository` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/SetRepository.swift`

### Implementation Notes
- `PREvaluationResult` and `PRTableEntry` are `Sendable` structs — safe to pass across actor boundaries
- `PRServiceProtocol` conforms to `Sendable` and declares all methods as `async throws`
- Contract source of truth: `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/contracts/PRServiceProtocol.swift`
- `fetchBestEligibleSet()` uses exerciseId+reps predicate in SwiftData `#Predicate`, then filters in Swift for hasData/excludeFromPRs/setType/excludingSetId (due to `#Predicate` limitations with complex conditionals)
- Sort by effectiveWeight DESC, date ASC — return first (earliest-highest wins per specdoc S4.2)
- The `Protocols/` directory under `Services/` must be created

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001/T002 (protocol file) and T003/T004 (repository update) are independent and can proceed in parallel

### Dependencies
- None (starting package). Requires feature 002 repositories to exist.

### Risks & Mitigations
- SwiftData `#Predicate` may not support filtering by `setType` against an exclusion set (warmup/partial) → Fall back to post-fetch Swift filtering on the small exerciseId+reps result set. Document in code comments.

---

## Work Package WP02: Core PR Pipeline — evaluate() (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Implement the PRService actor and the core `evaluate()` method that runs at write-time after every set save. This is the heart of the PR pipeline (specdoc S7.2 "On New Set Saved") — eligibility checking, PR lookup, new PR creation, beats-PR logic, exact-match with same-workout detection, and below-PR handling. All weight comparisons use integer grams via `UnitConversion.toGrams()`.
**Independent Test**: PRService.evaluate() correctly handles all 5 acceptance scenarios from User Story 1 in the spec: first PR creation, beats existing PR, exact match in different workout, exact match in same workout, and below PR. Eligibility filtering excludes sets with hasData=false, excludeFromPRs=true, warmup (when setting is off), and partial (always).
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/tasks/WP02-core-evaluate-pipeline.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T005 Create `PRService` actor skeleton in `Reppo/Core/Services/PRService.swift` with repository dependencies via initializer injection
- [x] T006 Implement eligibility check private method (hasData, excludeFromPRs, setType, warmup setting from HealthProfile) — specdoc S7.2 step 1
- [x] T007 Implement `evaluate()` method — full pipeline: PR lookup, new PR creation (step 3), beats-PR with old set demotion (step 4), exact-match with same-workout detection (step 5), below-PR (step 6) — specdoc S7.2
- [x] T008 Implement same-workout matching logic — compare workoutId of new set vs PR-owning set (specdoc S7.3)
- [x] T009 Implement integer grams comparison helper using `UnitConversion.toGrams()` — specdoc S8.3, FR-002

### Implementation Notes
- PRService is a plain Swift `actor` (NOT `@ModelActor`) — it never accesses ModelContext directly
- Dependencies injected via init: `PerformanceRecordRepositoryProtocol`, `SetRepositoryProtocol`, `HealthProfileRepositoryProtocol`, `ExerciseRepositoryProtocol`
- `evaluate()` follows specdoc S7.2 "On New Set Saved" step by step — the spec pseudocode is the implementation guide
- Eligibility check reads `HealthProfile.includeWarmupsInPRs` via `healthProfileRepo.fetchOrCreate()`
- All weight comparisons: `toGrams(effectiveWeight) > toGrams(existingPR.value)` — NEVER raw float comparison
- When new set beats PR: fetch old PR-owning set via `setRepo.fetch(byId:)`, update its cachedPRStatus to "previous", update PerformanceRecord fields (value, setId, date), save both
- When exact match: always store "matched" in DB (specdoc S7.3). UI hides badge for same-workout sets. Do NOT update PerformanceRecord (earliest wins).
- Return `PREvaluationResult` with all status changes for UI updates

### Parallel Opportunities
- T005 (actor skeleton) must come first
- T006 and T009 are independent utility methods, can proceed in parallel
- T007 and T008 are tightly coupled (same-workout logic is part of evaluate flow)

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (protocol and repository update must exist).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Cross-actor data flow: PRService receives UUIDs/primitives, fetches full models via repos. SwiftData @Model objects are NOT Sendable — never pass them between actors.
- HealthProfile read on every evaluate() call adds latency → Acceptable: single-row table, O(1) fetch. If needed later, cache in PRService actor state with invalidation.
- PerformanceRecord mutation: update existing record in-place (mutate properties + save), not delete+insert. Per specdoc S7.2 "UPDATE PerformanceRecord SET..."

---

## Work Package WP03: Edit/Delete Recomputation + Suffix-Max Display (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement `evaluateAfterEdit()` for PR recomputation when a set is edited, `handleDeletion()` for PR recovery when a PR-owning set is deleted, and `fetchPRTable()` with suffix-max filtering for display. These complete the reactive PR pipeline and display layer.
**Independent Test**: Editing a PR-owning set to a lower weight correctly promotes the next best candidate. Deleting the only set for a rep count removes the PerformanceRecord. Suffix-max filtering on the example from specdoc S7.4 produces the correct capability frontier (hides 10-rep at 85kg when 12-rep is 90kg).
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/tasks/WP03-edit-delete-and-display.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T010 Implement `evaluateAfterEdit()` — handles both non-owner (re-run evaluate) and owner cases (keep/update PR or find new winner) — specdoc S7.2 "On Set Edited"
- [x] T011 Implement `handleDeletion()` — find next best via `fetchBestEligibleSet()`, promote winner or delete PerformanceRecord — specdoc S7.2 "On Set Deleted"
- [x] T012 Implement `fetchPRTable()` — fetch all repMax records for exercise, apply suffix-max algorithm, return `[PRTableEntry]` — specdoc S7.4

### Implementation Notes
- `evaluateAfterEdit()`:
  - If `previousCachedPRStatus != .current` → re-run evaluate() logic with new values (treat as new set)
  - If was PR owner AND `toGrams(newWeight) >= toGrams(PR.value)` → update PR record with new value, keep "current"
  - If was PR owner AND `toGrams(newWeight) < toGrams(PR.value)` → call `fetchBestEligibleSet()` to find new winner
    - Winner != this set → update PR to winner, set winner to "current", re-evaluate this set
    - This set still wins (no other sets or still highest) → update PR with lower value, keep "current"
- `handleDeletion()`:
  - If `cachedPRStatus != .current` → return empty result (no work needed)
  - If was PR owner → `fetchBestEligibleSet(excludingSetId: deletedSetId)`
    - Winner found → update PR to winner, winner gets "current"
    - No sets remain → delete PerformanceRecord
- `fetchPRTable()`:
  - Fetch via `performanceRecordRepo.fetchAll(for: exerciseId, recordType: .repMax)`
  - Sort by reps DESC
  - Iterate with `maxWeightSeen` tracking — include only entries where `toGrams(value) > toGrams(maxWeightSeen)`
  - Return as `[PRTableEntry]` sorted by reps ASC for display

### Parallel Opportunities
- T010 and T011 are independent methods (edit vs delete paths)
- T012 (display) is fully independent of T010/T011

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (PRService actor and evaluate() must exist — evaluateAfterEdit delegates to evaluate logic).

### Risks & Mitigations
- `evaluateAfterEdit` for non-owner case: must handle the scenario where reps changed (old reps PR unaffected, new reps may create/beat a different PR). The simplest correct approach: if not owner, just call evaluate() with new values.
- `handleDeletion` race condition: the set is being deleted by the caller. PRService must update PerformanceRecord BEFORE the set is actually deleted from the database. Coordinate with future SetService.
- Suffix-max must use integer grams for weight comparison to be consistent with the rest of the pipeline.

---

## Work Package WP04: Bulk Rebuild + DI Wiring (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Implement `rebuild(for:)` and `rebuildAll()` for bulk PR reconstruction after import or settings changes, and wire PRService into the app's dependency injection. This completes the PRService feature.
**Independent Test**: `rebuildAll()` correctly reconstructs all PRs from raw sets. After toggling `includeWarmupsInPRs` and running rebuild, warmup sets are correctly included/excluded. PRService is accessible from the app's DI container.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/003-pr-service/tasks/WP04-rebuild-and-wiring.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~400 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T013 Implement `rebuild(for:)` — single exercise PR rebuild from scratch (delete PRs, find best per rep count, create records, update statuses)
- [x] T014 Implement `rebuildAll()` — iterate all exercises via `exerciseRepo.fetchAll()`, call `rebuild(for:)` per exercise
- [x] T015 Create `ServiceContainer` (or extend `RepositoryContainer`) for DI wiring — instantiate PRService with repository dependencies
- [x] T016 Wire PRService into `ReppoApp.swift` — inject via SwiftUI environment alongside repositories

### Implementation Notes
- `rebuild(for: exerciseId)`:
  1. Read `HealthProfile.includeWarmupsInPRs` setting
  2. Delete all PerformanceRecords for this exercise (`fetchAll` + delete each)
  3. Fetch all eligible sets for this exercise (use fetchSets + filter, grouped by reps)
  4. For each unique rep count: find best eligible set → create PerformanceRecord with "current"
  5. For remaining eligible sets: evaluate matches (same-workout rule applies) → set "matched" or null
  6. Clear `cachedPRStatus` on all ineligible sets for this exercise
- `rebuildAll()`:
  1. Read warmup setting once at start
  2. `exerciseRepo.fetchAll()` to get all exercises
  3. For each exercise: `rebuild(for: exercise.id)`
- **DI wiring**: Create a `ServiceContainer` class (or similar) that holds PRService. Initialize it after `RepositoryContainer` with repository references. Inject into SwiftUI environment.
- Service container pattern should be extensible for future services (SetService, StatsService in feature 004)

### Parallel Opportunities
- T013 and T014 are sequential (rebuildAll calls rebuild)
- T015 and T016 (DI wiring) are independent of T013/T014 but logically done last

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP03 (all PRService methods must exist for the service to be complete before wiring).

### Risks & Mitigations
- `rebuildAll()` performance on large datasets (50k+ sets) → Acceptable for rare operation. Not in hot path. Could add progress reporting in future if needed.
- `rebuild(for:)` must clear ALL cachedPRStatus for the exercise before re-evaluating → Requires fetching all sets, not just eligible ones, to clear old statuses. Use `setRepo.fetchSets(for: exerciseId, limit: nil)`.
- DI wiring: `ServiceContainer` holds services only. It receives `RepositoryContainer` in its init to wire service dependencies. Two separate containers (repos + services) follows the existing pattern.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 → WP02 → WP03 → WP04 (linear dependency chain)
- **Parallelization**: Within WP01, protocol creation and repository update are independent. Within WP03, all 3 methods are independent. WP04 is the integration package.
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 constitute the minimum viable PR service — protocols, evaluate() on new sets, and the repository query. This handles the most common case (new set saved).
- **Full Scope**: WP01 + WP02 + WP03 + WP04 = complete PR service with all pipeline paths, display, rebuild, and DI wiring.

---
